    50→# SQL schema definition
    51→SCHEMA = """
    52→-- WAL mode for concurrent reads during writes
    53→PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL;
    54→PRAGMA foreign_keys = ON;
    55→-- NOTE: Existing databases may still have agent_id FK constraints in events, notes, merge_queue.
    56→-- Deletion code must use PRAGMA foreign_keys = OFF when deleting agents for backward compatibility.
    57→PRAGMA busy_timeout = 5000;
    58→
    59→----------------------------------------------------------------------
    60→-- ISSUES: the universal work unit
    61→----------------------------------------------------------------------
    62→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS issues (
    63→    id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
    64→    title       TEXT NOT NULL,
    65→    description TEXT,
    66→    status      TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'open',
    67→    priority    INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 2,
    68→    type        TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'task',
    69→    assignee    TEXT,
    70→    parent_id   TEXT REFERENCES issues(id),
    71→    project     TEXT,
    72→    model       TEXT,
    73→    tags        TEXT,  -- JSON array of tag strings
    74→    metadata    TEXT,
    75→    created_at  TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    76→    updated_at  TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    77→    closed_at   TEXT
    78→);
    79→
    80→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_status ON issues(status);
    81→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_assignee ON issues(assignee);
    82→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_parent ON issues(parent_id);
    83→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_project ON issues(project);
    84→----------------------------------------------------------------------
    85→-- DEPENDENCIES: edges in the work DAG
    86→----------------------------------------------------------------------
    87→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS dependencies (
    88→    issue_id    TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES issues(id),
    89→    depends_on  TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES issues(id),
    90→    type        TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'blocks',
    91→    created_at  TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
    92→    PRIMARY KEY (issue_id, depends_on)
    93→);
    94→
    95→----------------------------------------------------------------------
    96→-- AGENTS: ephemeral execution identity (deleted after merge/cleanup)
    97→----------------------------------------------------------------------
    98→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS agents (
    99→    id          TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   100→    name        TEXT NOT NULL,
   101→    status      TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'idle',
   102→    session_id  TEXT,
   103→    worktree    TEXT,
   104→    current_issue TEXT REFERENCES issues(id),
   105→    project     TEXT,
   106→    model       TEXT,
   107→    lease_expires_at TEXT,
   108→    last_progress_at TEXT,
   109→    last_heartbeat_at TEXT,
   110→    metadata    TEXT,
   111→    created_at  TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
   112→    updated_at  TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
   113→);
   114→
   115→----------------------------------------------------------------------
   116→-- EVENTS: append-only audit trail
   117→-- agent_id is a correlation key, not a live FK (agents are deleted after merge)
   118→----------------------------------------------------------------------
   119→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS events (
   120→    id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
   121→    issue_id    TEXT REFERENCES issues(id),
   122→    agent_id    TEXT,
   123→    event_type  TEXT NOT NULL,
   124→    detail      TEXT,
   125→    created_at  TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
   126→);
   127→
   128→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_issue ON events(issue_id);
   129→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_agent ON events(agent_id);
   130→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_events_type ON events(event_type);
   131→
   132→----------------------------------------------------------------------
   133→-- NOTES: inter-agent knowledge transfer system
   134→----------------------------------------------------------------------
   135→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS notes (
   136→    id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
   137→    issue_id    TEXT REFERENCES issues(id),
   138→    agent_id    TEXT,
   139→    project     TEXT,
   140→    category    TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'discovery',
   141→    content     TEXT NOT NULL,
   142→    created_at  TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
   143→);
   144→
   145→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_notes_issue ON notes(issue_id);
   146→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_notes_category ON notes(category);
   147→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_notes_created ON notes(created_at);
   148→
   149→----------------------------------------------------------------------
   150→-- NOTE_DELIVERIES: delivery tracking for notes
   151→----------------------------------------------------------------------
   152→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS note_deliveries (
   153→    id INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
   154→    note_id INTEGER NOT NULL REFERENCES notes(id),
   155→    recipient_agent_id TEXT,
   156→    recipient_issue_id TEXT,
   157→    status TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'queued',
   158→    delivered_at TEXT,
   159→    read_at TEXT,
   160→    acked_at TEXT,
   161→    created_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now'))
   162→);
   163→
   164→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_note_deliveries_note ON note_deliveries(note_id);
   165→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_note_deliveries_inbox ON note_deliveries(recipient_agent_id, recipient_issue_id, status, created_at);
   166→CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS uidx_note_deliveries_note_agent_global ON note_deliveries(note_id, recipient_agent_id) WHERE recipient_issue_id IS NULL;
   167→CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS uidx_note_deliveries_note_agent_issue ON note_deliveries(note_id, recipient_agent_id, recipient_issue_id) WHERE recipient_issue_id IS NOT NULL;
   168→CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS uidx_note_deliveries_note_issue_target ON note_deliveries(note_id, recipient_issue_id) WHERE recipient_agent_id IS NULL;
   169→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_note_deliveries_issue_targets ON note_deliveries(recipient_issue_id) WHERE recipient_agent_id IS NULL;
   170→
   171→----------------------------------------------------------------------
   172→-- MERGE_QUEUE: dedicated finalizer queue
   173→----------------------------------------------------------------------
   174→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS merge_queue (
   175→    id          INTEGER PRIMARY KEY AUTOINCREMENT,
   176→    issue_id    TEXT NOT NULL REFERENCES issues(id),
   177→    agent_id    TEXT,
   178→    project     TEXT NOT NULL,
   179→    worktree    TEXT NOT NULL,
   180→    branch_name TEXT NOT NULL,
   181→    test_command TEXT,
   182→    status      TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT 'queued',
   183→    enqueued_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (datetime('now')),
   184→    completed_at TEXT
   185→);
   186→
   187→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_mq_status ON merge_queue(status);
   188→CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_mq_project ON merge_queue(project);
   189→
   190→----------------------------------------------------------------------
   191→-- PROJECTS: registry of all known projects and their disk paths
   192→----------------------------------------------------------------------
   193→CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS projects (
   194→    name        TEXT PRIMARY KEY,
   195→    path        TEXT NOT NULL,
   196→    registered_at TEXT NOT NULL DEFAULT (strftime('%Y-%m-%d %H:%M:%S', 'now'))
   197→);
   198→
   199→----------------------------------------------------------------------
   200→-- AGENT_RUNS: Materialized view over events for per-agent-run metrics
   201→----------------------------------------------------------------------
   202→CREATE VIEW IF NOT EXISTS agent_runs AS
   203→SELECT
   204→    e_start.agent_id,
   205→    e_start.issue_id,
   206→    i.type as issue_type,
   207→    COALESCE(i.model, 'unknown') as model,
   208→    i.tags,
   209→    CASE
   210→        WHEN e_done.id IS NOT NULL THEN 'done'
   211→        WHEN e_inc.id IS NOT NULL THEN 'incomplete'
   212→        WHEN e_esc.id IS NOT NULL THEN 'escalated'
   213→        ELSE 'unknown'
   214→    END as outcome,
   215→    ROUND((julianday(COALESCE(e_done.created_at, e_inc.created_at, e_esc.created_at)) - julianday(e_start.created_at)) * 86400, 1) as duration_s,
   216→    (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM events er WHERE er.issue_id = e_start.issue_id AND er.event_type = 'retry') as retry_count,
   217→    (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM events en WHERE en.agent_id = e_start.agent_id AND en.event_type = 'notes_harvested') as notes_produced,
   218→    (SELECT COUNT(*) FROM events eni WHERE eni.agent_id = e_start.agent_id AND eni.event_type = 'notes_injected') as notes_injected,
   219→    e_start.created_at as started_at,
   220→    COALESCE(e_done.created_at, e_inc.created_at, e_esc.created_at) as ended_at
   221→FROM events e_start
   222→JOIN issues i ON e_start.issue_id = i.id
   223→LEFT JOIN events e_done ON e_start.agent_id = e_done.agent_id AND e_done.event_type = 'completed'
   224→LEFT JOIN events e_inc ON e_start.agent_id = e_inc.agent_id AND e_inc.event_type = 'incomplete'
   225→LEFT JOIN events e_esc ON e_start.issue_id = e_esc.issue_id AND e_esc.event_type = 'escalated'
   226→WHERE e_start.event_type = 'worker_started';
   227→
   228→----------------------------------------------------------------------
   229→
   230→"""
   231→
   232→
   233→class DatabaseCore:
   234→    """SQLite database wrapper for Hive orchestrator."""
   235→
   236→    def __init__(self, db_path: str = None):
   237→        """
   238→        Initialize database connection.
   239→
   240→        Args:
   241→            db_path: Path to SQLite database file
   242→        """
   243→        from ..config import Config
   244→
   245→        self.db_path = db_path or Config.DB_PATH
   246→        self.conn: Optional[sqlite3.Connection] = None
   247→
   248→    def connect(self):
   249→        """Open database connection and initialize schema."""
   250→        Path(self.db_path).parent.mkdir(parents=True, exist_ok=True)
   251→        self.conn = sqlite3.connect(self.db_path, check_same_thread=False, timeout=10)
   252→        self.conn.row_factory = sqlite3.Row
   253→        self.conn.execute("PRAGMA journal_mode = WAL")
   254→        self._init_schema()
   255→
   256→    def _init_schema(self):
   257→        """Create tables/views and run migrations.
   258→
   259→        Skipped when the DB is already initialized (tables exist) and the
   260→        write lock cannot be acquired — keeps CLI commands fast and
   261→        non-blocking when the daemon holds a transaction.
   262→        """
   263→        # Check if schema already exists (issues table is a reliable marker)
   264→        cursor = self.conn.execute("SELECT name FROM sqlite_master WHERE type='table' AND name='issues'")
   265→        schema_exists = cursor.fetchone() is not None
   266→
   267→        if schema_exists:
   268→            # Best-effort: refresh views + migrations if we can grab the lock,
   269→            # but don't block CLI reads if the daemon is busy.
   270→            try:
   271→                self.conn.execute("DROP VIEW IF EXISTS agent_runs")
   272→                self.conn.executescript(SCHEMA)
   273→                self._migrate_if_needed()
   274→            except sqlite3.OperationalError:
   275→                pass  # daemon has the lock — schema is already current
   276→        else:
   277→            # First-time init: must succeed.
   278→            self.conn.executescript(SCHEMA)
   279→            self._migrate_if_needed()
   280→
   281→    def close(self):
   282→        """Close database connection."""
   283→        if self.conn:
   284→            self.conn.close()
   285→            self.conn = None
   286→
   287→    @contextmanager
   288→    def transaction(self):
   289→        """Context manager for database transactions."""
   290→        if not self.conn:
   291→            raise RuntimeError("Database not connected")
   292→        try:
   293→            yield self.conn
   294→            self.conn.commit()
   295→        except Exception:
   296→            self.conn.rollback()
   297→            raise
   298→
   299→    def _ensure_column(self, table: str, column: str, col_type: str):
   300→        """Add column to table if it does not exist. Idempotent."""
   301→        cursor = self.conn.execute(f"PRAGMA table_info({table})")
   302→        columns = [row[1] for row in cursor.fetchall()]
   303→        if column not in columns:
   304→            try:
   305→                self.conn.execute(f"ALTER TABLE {table} ADD COLUMN {column} {col_type}")
   306→                self.conn.commit()
   307→                logger.info(f"Added {column} column to {table} table")
   308→            except sqlite3.Error as e:
   309→                if "duplicate column name" not in str(e).lower():
   310→                    raise
   311→
   312→    def _migrate_if_needed(self):
   313→        """Apply any necessary database migrations."""
   314→        if not self.conn:
   315→            raise RuntimeError("Database not connected")
   316→
   317→        # Add missing columns using helper
   318→        self._ensure_column("issues", "model", "TEXT")
   319→        self._ensure_column("issues", "tags", "TEXT")
   320→        self._ensure_column("merge_queue", "test_command", "TEXT")
   321→        self._ensure_column("notes", "project", "TEXT")
   322→        self._ensure_column("agents", "project", "TEXT")
   323→        self._ensure_column("notes", "must_read", "INTEGER NOT NULL DEFAULT 0")
   324→        self._ensure_column("agents", "last_heartbeat_at", "TEXT")
   325→
   326→        # Create tags index (safe after column exists via CREATE TABLE or migration)
   327→        self.conn.execute("CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_issues_tags ON issues(tags)")
   328→        self.conn.commit()
   329→
   330→        # Backfill notes.project from issues.project via issue_id FK (always run to catch any NULL values)
   331→        self.conn.execute("""
   332→            UPDATE notes
   333→            SET project = (SELECT project FROM issues WHERE issues.id = notes.issue_id)
   334→            WHERE issue_id IS NOT NULL AND project IS NULL
   335→        """)
   336→        if self.conn.total_changes > 0:
   337→            self.conn.commit()
   338→            logger.info(f"Backfilled {self.conn.total_changes} notes.project from issues.project")
   339→
   340→        # Create index on notes.project if it doesn't exist
   341→        self.conn.execute("CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_notes_project ON notes(project)")
   342→        self.conn.commit()
   343→
   344→        # Backfill heartbeat for pre-migration agent rows.
   345→        # Use a local delta so logging isn't polluted by prior statements on this connection.
   346→        before_changes = self.conn.total_changes
   347→        self.conn.execute(
   348→            """
   349→            UPDATE agents
   350→            SET last_heartbeat_at = COALESCE(last_progress_at, datetime('now'))
   351→            WHERE last_heartbeat_at IS NULL
   352→            """
   353→        )
   354→        backfilled = self.conn.total_changes - before_changes
   355→        if backfilled > 0:
   356→            self.conn.commit()
   357→            logger.info(f"Backfilled {backfilled} agents.last_heartbeat_at")
   358→
   359→        # Collapse 'failed' issue status into 'escalated'
   360→        self.conn.execute("UPDATE issues SET status = 'escalated' WHERE status = 'failed'")
   361→        self.conn.commit()
   362→
   363→        self._ensure_merge_queue_idempotency()
   364→
   365→        # Create index on agents.project if it doesn't exist
   366→        self.conn.execute("CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_agents_project ON agents(project)")
   367→        self.conn.commit()
   368→
   369→    def _ensure_merge_queue_idempotency(self) -> None:
   370→        """Ensure merge queue constraints that make enqueueing idempotent.
   371→
   372→        Invariant: there can be at most one active merge entry (queued|running)
   373→        per issue. This prevents duplicate merge enqueues when completion
   374→        handling is replayed.
   375→        """
   376→        if not self.conn:
   377→            raise RuntimeError("Database not connected")
   378→
   379→        index_sql = """
   380→            CREATE UNIQUE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS uidx_merge_queue_active_issue
   381→            ON merge_queue(issue_id)
   382→            WHERE status IN ('queued', 'running')
   383→        """
   384→
   385→        try:
   386→            self.conn.execute(index_sql)
   387→            self.conn.commit()
   388→            return
   389→        except sqlite3.Error as e:
   390→            if "unique constraint failed" not in str(e).lower():
   391→                raise
   392→
   393→        logger.warning("Deduping merge_queue active entries before adding unique index")
   394→        self._dedupe_active_merge_queue_entries()
   395→        self.conn.execute(index_sql)
   396→        self.conn.commit()
   397→
   398→    def _dedupe_active_merge_queue_entries(self) -> None:
   399→        """Best-effort: collapse duplicate active merge entries per issue."""
   400→        if not self.conn:
   401→            raise RuntimeError("Database not connected")
   402→
   403→        dup_rows = self.conn.execute(
   404→            """
   405→            SELECT issue_id
   406→            FROM merge_queue
   407→            WHERE status IN ('queued', 'running')
   408→            GROUP BY issue_id
   409→            HAVING COUNT(*) > 1
   410→            """
   411→        ).fetchall()
   412→
   413→        if not dup_rows:
   414→            return
   415→
   416→        for row in dup_rows:
   417→            issue_id = row["issue_id"]
   418→            entries = self.conn.execute(
   419→                """
   420→                SELECT id, status, enqueued_at
   421→                FROM merge_queue
   422→                WHERE issue_id = ?
   423→                  AND status IN ('queued', 'running')
   424→                ORDER BY
   425→                  CASE status WHEN 'running' THEN 0 ELSE 1 END,
   426→                  enqueued_at ASC,
   427→                  id ASC
   428→                """,
   429→                (issue_id,),
   430→            ).fetchall()
   431→
   432→            if len(entries) <= 1:
   433→                continue
   434→
   435→            keep_id = entries[0]["id"]
   436→            drop_ids = [e["id"] for e in entries[1:]]
   437→            logger.warning(f"merge_queue dedupe: keeping {keep_id} and failing {drop_ids} for issue {issue_id}")
   438→            self.conn.executemany(
   439→                "UPDATE merge_queue SET status = 'failed', completed_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?",
   440→                [(merge_id,) for merge_id in drop_ids],
   441→            )
   442→
   443→        self.conn.commit()
   444→
   445→    def enqueue_merge(
   446→        self,
   447→        *,
   448→        issue_id: str,
   449→        agent_id: Optional[str],
   450→        project: str,
   451→        worktree: str,
   452→        branch_name: str,
   453→        test_command: Optional[str] = None,
   454→    ) -> bool:
   455→        """Enqueue an issue for merge processing.
   456→
   457→        Returns:
   458→            True if a new merge_queue row was inserted, False if it already existed.
   459→        """
   460→        with self.transaction() as conn:
   461→            cursor = conn.execute(
   462→                """
   463→                INSERT OR IGNORE INTO merge_queue (issue_id, agent_id, project, worktree, branch_name, test_command)
   464→                VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
   465→                """,
   466→                (issue_id, agent_id, project, worktree, branch_name, test_command),
   467→            )
   468→            return cursor.rowcount == 1
   469→
   470→    def try_transition_merge_queue_status(
   471→        self,
   472→        queue_id: int,
   473→        *,
   474→        from_status: str,
   475→        to_status: str,
   476→        completed_at: Optional[str] = None,
   477→    ) -> bool:
   478→        """CAS-style merge_queue status transition.
   479→
   480→        Returns:
   481→            True if updated, False if the entry was not in from_status.
   482→        """
   483→        with self.transaction() as conn:
   484→            cursor = conn.execute(
   485→                """
   486→                UPDATE merge_queue
   487→                SET status = ?, completed_at = ?
   488→                WHERE id = ? AND status = ?
   489→                """,
   490→                (to_status, completed_at, queue_id, from_status),
   491→            )
   492→            return cursor.rowcount == 1
   493→
   494→    def log_event(
   495→        self,
   496→        issue_id: Optional[str],
   497→        agent_id: Optional[str],
   498→        event_type: str,
   499→        detail: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
   500→        commit: bool = True,
   501→    ):
   502→        """
   503→        Log an event to the audit trail.
   504→
   505→        Args:
   506→            issue_id: Related issue ID (optional)
   507→            agent_id: Related agent ID (optional)
   508→            event_type: Type of event (created, claimed, done, etc.)
   509→            detail: Additional event details dict
   510→        """
   511→        detail_json = json.dumps(detail) if detail else None
   512→
   513→        if not self.conn:
   514→            raise RuntimeError("Database not connected")
   515→
   516→        self.conn.execute(
   517→            """
   518→            INSERT INTO events (issue_id, agent_id, event_type, detail)
   519→            VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?)
   520→            """,
   521→            (issue_id, agent_id, event_type, detail_json),
   522→        )
   523→        if commit:
   524→            self.conn.commit()
   525→
   526→    def create_agent(
   527→        self,
   528→        name: str,
   529→        model: str = "claude-sonnet-4-5-20250929",
   530→        metadata: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
   531→        project: Optional[str] = None,
   532→    ) -> str:
   533→        """
   534→        Create a new agent identity.
   535→
   536→        Args:
   537→            name: Human-readable agent name
   538→            model: Model identifier
   539→            metadata: Additional metadata dict
   540→            project: Project identifier (optional)
   541→
   542→        Returns:
   543→            Generated agent ID
   544→        """
   545→        agent_id = generate_id("agent")
   546→        metadata_json = json.dumps(metadata) if metadata else None
   547→
   548→        with self.transaction() as conn:
   549→            conn.execute(
   550→                """
   551→                INSERT INTO agents (id, name, model, metadata, project)
   552→                VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
   553→                """,
   554→                (agent_id, name, model, metadata_json, project),
   555→            )
   556→
   557→        return agent_id
   558→
   559→    def get_agent(self, agent_id: str) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:
   560→        """Get agent by ID."""
   561→        cursor = self.conn.execute("SELECT * FROM agents WHERE id = ?", (agent_id,))
   562→        row = cursor.fetchone()
   563→        return dict(row) if row else None
   564→
   565→    def try_transition_agent_status(
   566→        self,
   567→        agent_id: str,
   568→        *,
   569→        from_status: str,
   570→        to_status: str,
   571→    ) -> bool:
   572→        """CAS-style agent status transition."""
   573→        with self.transaction() as conn:
   574→            cursor = conn.execute(
   575→                """
   576→                UPDATE agents
   577→                SET status = ?,
   578→                    updated_at = datetime('now')
   579→                WHERE id = ? AND status = ?
   580→                """,
   581→                (to_status, agent_id, from_status),
   582→            )
   583→            return cursor.rowcount == 1
   584→
   585→    def try_touch_agent_heartbeat(
   586→        self,
   587→        agent_id: str,
   588→        *,
   589→        required_status: str = "working",
   590→    ) -> bool:
   591→        """Update an agent heartbeat if it is in the expected status."""
   592→        with self.transaction() as conn:
   593→            cursor = conn.execute(
   594→                """
   595→                UPDATE agents
   596→                SET last_heartbeat_at = datetime('now'),
   597→                    last_progress_at = datetime('now'),
   598→                    updated_at = datetime('now')
   599→                WHERE id = ? AND status = ?
   600→                """,
   601→                (agent_id, required_status),
   602→            )
   603→            return cursor.rowcount == 1
   604→
   605→    def get_active_agents(self, project: Optional[str] = None) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
   606→        """
   607→        Get all currently active agents.
   608→
   609→        Args:
   610→            project: Filter by project (optional)
   611→
   612→        Returns:
   613→            List of active agent dicts
   614→        """
   615→        query = "SELECT * FROM agents WHERE status = 'working'"
   616→        params = []
   617→
   618→        if project is not None:
   619→            query += " AND project = ?"
   620→            params.append(project)
   621→
   622→        query += " ORDER BY created_at ASC"
   623→
   624→        cursor = self.conn.execute(query, params)
   625→        return [dict(row) for row in cursor.fetchall()]
   626→
   627→    # --- Merge Queue Methods ---
   628→
   629→    def get_queued_merges(self, project: Optional[str] = None, limit: int = 10) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
   630→        """
   631→        Get queued merge queue entries, oldest first.
   632→
   633→        Args:
   634→            project: Filter by project (optional)
   635→            limit: Maximum entries to return
   636→
   637→        Returns:
   638→            List of merge queue entry dicts with joined issue/agent info
   639→        """
   640→        query = """
   641→            SELECT mq.*, i.title as issue_title, a.name as agent_name
   642→            FROM merge_queue mq
   643→            JOIN issues i ON mq.issue_id = i.id
   644→            LEFT JOIN agents a ON mq.agent_id = a.id
   645→            WHERE mq.status = 'queued'
   646→        """
   647→
   648→        params = []
   649→        if project is not None:
   650→            query += " AND mq.project = ?"
   651→            params.append(project)
   652→
   653→        query += " ORDER BY mq.enqueued_at ASC LIMIT ?"
   654→        params.append(limit)
   655→
   656→        cursor = self.conn.execute(query, params)
   657→        return [dict(row) for row in cursor.fetchall()]
   658→
   659→    def get_merge_queue_stats(self) -> Dict[str, int]:
   660→        """
   661→        Get merge queue statistics by status.
   662→
   663→        Returns:
   664→            Dict mapping status to count, e.g. {"queued": 3, "merged": 10, ...}
   665→        """
   666→        cursor = self.conn.execute(
   667→            """
   668→            SELECT status, COUNT(*) as count
   669→            FROM merge_queue
   670→            GROUP BY status
   671→            """
   672→        )
   673→        stats = {"queued": 0, "running": 0, "merged": 0, "failed": 0}
   674→        for row in cursor.fetchall():
   675→            stats[row["status"]] = row["count"]
   676→        return stats
   677→
   678→    def log_system_event(self, event_type: str, detail: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None, commit: bool = True):
   679→        """
   680→        Log a system-level event to the audit trail.
   681→
   682→        Args:
   683→            event_type: Type of system event (e.g., 'daemon_started')
   684→            detail: Additional event details dict
   685→        """
   686→        detail_json = json.dumps(detail) if detail else None
   687→
   688→        if not self.conn:
   689→            raise RuntimeError("Database not connected")
   690→
   691→        self.conn.execute(
   692→            """
   693→            INSERT INTO events (issue_id, agent_id, event_type, detail)
   694→            VALUES (NULL, NULL, ?, ?)
   695→            """,
   696→            (event_type, detail_json),
   697→        )
   698→        if commit:
   699→            self.conn.commit()
   700→
   701→    def _query_events(
   702→        self,
   703→        *,
   704→        after_id: int = 0,
   705→        issue_id: Optional[str] = None,
   706→        agent_id: Optional[str] = None,
   707→        event_type: Optional[str] = None,
   708→        limit: Optional[int] = None,
   709→        order: str = "DESC",
   710→    ) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
   711→        """Private helper for querying events with optional filtering and ordering."""
   712→        conditions = []
   713→        params = []
   714→        if after_id:
   715→            conditions.append("id > ?")
   716→            params.append(after_id)
   717→        if issue_id:
   718→            conditions.append("issue_id = ?")
   719→            params.append(issue_id)
   720→        if agent_id:
   721→            conditions.append("agent_id = ?")
   722→            params.append(agent_id)
   723→        if event_type:
   724→            conditions.append("event_type = ?")
   725→            params.append(event_type)
   726→        where = ("WHERE " + " AND ".join(conditions)) if conditions else ""
   727→        query = f"SELECT * FROM events {where} ORDER BY id {order}"
   728→        if limit:
   729→            query += " LIMIT ?"
   730→            params.append(limit)
   731→        cursor = self.conn.execute(query, params)
   732→        return [dict(row) for row in cursor.fetchall()]
   733→
   734→    def get_events(
   735→        self,
   736→        issue_id: Optional[str] = None,
   737→        agent_id: Optional[str] = None,
   738→        event_type: Optional[str] = None,
   739→        limit: int = 100,
   740→    ) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
   741→        """Get events filtered by issue, agent, or type."""
   742→        return self._query_events(issue_id=issue_id, agent_id=agent_id, event_type=event_type, limit=limit, order="DESC")
   743→
   744→    def get_events_since(
   745→        self,
   746→        after_id: int = 0,
   747→        issue_id: Optional[str] = None,
   748→        agent_id: Optional[str] = None,
   749→        event_type: Optional[str] = None,
   750→    ) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
   751→        """Get events with id > after_id, ordered ascending (oldest first)."""
   752→        return self._query_events(after_id=after_id, issue_id=issue_id, agent_id=agent_id, event_type=event_type, order="ASC")
   753→
   754→    def get_max_event_id(self) -> int:
   755→        """Return the current maximum event id, or 0 if no events."""
   756→        cursor = self.conn.execute("SELECT COALESCE(MAX(id), 0) FROM events")
   757→        return cursor.fetchone()[0]
   758→
   759→    def get_recent_events(
   760→        self,
   761→        n: int = 20,
   762→        issue_id: Optional[str] = None,
   763→        agent_id: Optional[str] = None,
   764→        event_type: Optional[str] = None,
   765→    ) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
   766→        """Get the most recent n events, returned oldest-first."""
   767→        results = self._query_events(issue_id=issue_id, agent_id=agent_id, event_type=event_type, limit=n, order="DESC")
   768→        return list(reversed(results))
   769→
   770→    # ── Projects ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────
   771→
   772→    def register_project(self, name: str, path: str) -> None:
   773→        """Register a project name→path mapping. Idempotent via INSERT OR REPLACE.
   774→
   775→        INV-2: Normalizes *name* to the bare repo form — if a caller passes
   776→        "org/repo", it is stored as "repo" to match what detect_project() returns.
   777→        """
   778→        if not name:
   779→            raise ValueError("Project name must not be empty")
   780→        if not path:
   781→            raise ValueError("Project path must not be empty")
   782→        name = _normalize_project_name(name)
   783→        with self.transaction():
   784→            self.conn.execute(
   785→                "INSERT OR REPLACE INTO projects (name, path) VALUES (?, ?)",
   786→                (name, path),
   787→            )
   788→
   789→    def list_projects(self) -> list[dict]:
   790→        """Return all registered projects as [{name, path, registered_at}]."""
   791→        rows = self.conn.execute("SELECT name, path, registered_at FROM projects ORDER BY name").fetchall()
   792→        return [dict(r) for r in rows]
   793→
   794→    def get_project_path(self, name: str) -> Optional[str]:
   795→        """Return the disk path for a project by name, or None if not found."""
   796→        row = self.conn.execute("SELECT path FROM projects WHERE name = ?", (name,)).fetchone()
   797→        return row["path"] if row else None

    18→def _do_setup(project_path: Path, project_name: str, *, json_mode: bool = False):
    19→    """Write a default .hive.toml if one doesn't exist."""
    20→    import json
    21→
    22→    target = project_path / ".hive.toml"
    23→    if target.exists():
    24→        if json_mode:
    25→            print(json.dumps({"config_exists": True, "path": str(target)}))
    26→        else:
    27→            print(f"{target} already exists.")
    28→        return
    29→    target.write_text(f'[project]\nname = "{project_name}"\n\n[hive]\nbackend = "claude"\nmerge_queue_enabled = false\n')
    30→    if json_mode:
    31→        print(json.dumps({"config_created": str(target)}))
    32→    else:
    33→        print(f"Created {target}")
    34→

    37→def cli_command(*, formatter):
    38→    """Decorator that separates data-gathering from output formatting.
    39→
    40→    Decorated methods return a JSON-serialisable dict — nothing else.
    41→    The decorator routes the result to either ``json.dumps`` (when ``--json``
    42→    is active) or the provided *formatter* function (for human-readable text).
    43→
    44→    ``json_mode`` is popped from kwargs by the decorator so the wrapped
    45→    method never sees it.  Methods that handle output inline (e.g.
    46→    streaming follow mode) can read ``self._json_mode`` instead.
    47→    """
    48→
    49→    def decorator(fn):
    50→        @wraps(fn)
    51→        def wrapper(self, *args, **kwargs):
    52→            json_mode = kwargs.pop("json_mode", False)
    53→            self._json_mode = json_mode
    54→            try:
    55→                result = fn(self, *args, **kwargs)
    56→                if result is not None:
    57→                    if json_mode:
    58→                        print(json.dumps(result, default=str))
    59→                    else:
    60→                        output = formatter(result)
    61→                        if output:
    62→                            print(output)
    63→                return result
    64→            except Exception as e:
    65→                if json_mode:
    66→                    print(json.dumps({"error": str(e)}))
    67→                else:
    68→                    print(f"Error: {e}", file=sys.stderr)
    69→                sys.exit(1)
    70→
    71→        return wrapper
    72→
    73→    return decorator
    74→

    76→class HiveCLI(QueenMixin):
    77→    """Command-line interface for Hive orchestrator."""
    78→
    79→    def __init__(self, db: Database, project_path: str):
    80→        self.db = db
    81→        self.project_path = Path(project_path).resolve()
    82→        self.project_name = self.project_path.name
    83→
    84→    def _error(self, msg: str, *, json_mode: bool = False):
    85→        """Print error and exit."""
    86→        if json_mode:
    87→            print(json.dumps({"error": msg}))
    88→        else:
    89→            print(f"Error: {msg}", file=sys.stderr)
    90→        sys.exit(1)

    92→    def _parse_tags(self, issue_dict: dict) -> dict:
    93→        """Parse tags JSON string into a list in-place."""
    94→        if issue_dict.get("tags"):
    95→            try:
    96→                issue_dict["tags"] = json.loads(issue_dict["tags"])
    97→            except (json.JSONDecodeError, TypeError):
    98→                issue_dict["tags"] = []
    99→        else:
   100→            issue_dict["tags"] = []
   101→        return issue_dict

   103→    def _require_issue(self, issue_id: str) -> dict:
   104→        """Fetch issue by ID or raise ValueError if not found."""
   105→        issue = self.db.get_issue(issue_id)
   106→        if not issue:
   107→            raise ValueError(f"Issue not found: {issue_id}")
   108→        return issue
   109→
   110→    # Map user-facing sort names to SQL column names
   111→    _SORT_COLUMNS = {
   112→        "priority": "priority",
   113→        "created": "created_at",
   114→        "updated": "updated_at",
   115→        "status": "status",
   116→        "title": "title",
   117→    }

   121→    @cli_command(formatter=_fmt_create)
   122→    def create(
   123→        self,
   124→        title: str,
   125→        description: str = "",
   126→        priority: int = 2,
   127→        issue_type: str = "task",
   128→        model: Optional[str] = None,
   129→        tags: Optional[str] = None,
   130→        depends_on: Optional[list] = None,
   131→    ):
   132→        """Create a new issue."""
   133→        tag_list = [t.strip() for t in tags.split(",")] if tags else None
   134→        issue_id = self.db.create_issue(
   135→            title=title,
   136→            description=description,
   137→            priority=priority,
   138→            issue_type=issue_type,
   139→            project=self.project_name,
   140→            model=model,
   141→            tags=tag_list,
   142→            depends_on=depends_on,
   143→        )
   144→
   145→        return {
   146→            "id": issue_id,
   147→            "issue_id": issue_id,  # compat alias
   148→            "title": title,
   149→            "priority": priority,
   150→            "status": "open",
   151→            "tags": tag_list or [],
   152→            "depends_on": depends_on or [],
   153→            "message": f"Created issue {issue_id}: {title}",
   154→        }
   155→
   156→    _DONE_STATUSES = ("done", "finalized", "canceled")
   157→
   158→    @cli_command(formatter=_fmt_list_issues)
   159→    def list_issues(
   160→        self,
   161→        status: Optional[str] = None,
   162→        sort_by: str = "priority",
   163→        reverse: bool = False,
   164→        issue_type: Optional[str] = None,
   165→        assignee: Optional[str] = None,
   166→        limit: int = 50,
   167→        todo: bool = False,
   168→    ):
   169→        """List all issues."""
   170→        query = "SELECT * FROM issues WHERE project = ?"
   171→        params: List[Any] = [self.project_name]
   172→
   173→        if todo:
   174→            placeholders = ",".join("?" for _ in self._DONE_STATUSES)
   175→            query += f" AND status NOT IN ({placeholders})"
   176→            params.extend(self._DONE_STATUSES)
   177→        elif status:
   178→            query += " AND status = ?"
   179→            params.append(status)
   180→        if assignee:
   181→            query += " AND assignee = ?"
   182→            params.append(assignee)
   183→        if issue_type:
   184→            query += " AND type = ?"
   185→            params.append(issue_type)
   186→
   187→        # Resolve sort column (default to priority if unknown)
   188→        sort_col = self._SORT_COLUMNS.get(sort_by, "priority")
   189→        direction = "DESC" if reverse else "ASC"
   190→        query += f" ORDER BY {sort_col} {direction}"
   191→
   192→        query += " LIMIT ?"
   193→        params.append(str(limit))
   194→
   195→        cursor = self.db.conn.execute(query, params)
   196→        issues = []
   197→        for row in cursor.fetchall():
   198→            issue_dict = dict(row)
   199→            self._parse_tags(issue_dict)
   200→            issues.append(issue_dict)

200→            issues.append(issue_dict)
   201→
   202→        return {"count": len(issues), "issues": issues}

   204→    @cli_command(formatter=_fmt_show)
   205→    def show(self, issue_id: str):
   206→        """Show issue details and events."""
   207→        issue = self._require_issue(issue_id)
   208→
   209→        # Get dependencies
   210→        cursor = self.db.conn.execute(
   211→            """
   212→            SELECT i.id, i.title, i.status
   213→            FROM dependencies d
   214→            JOIN issues i ON d.depends_on = i.id
   215→            WHERE d.issue_id = ?
   216→            """,
   217→            (issue_id,),
   218→        )
   219→        dependencies = [dict(row) for row in cursor.fetchall()]
   220→
   221→        # Get dependents (issues blocked by this one)
   222→        cursor = self.db.conn.execute(
   223→            """
   224→            SELECT i.id, i.title, i.status
   225→            FROM dependencies d
   226→            JOIN issues i ON d.issue_id = i.id
   227→            WHERE d.depends_on = ?
   228→            """,
   229→            (issue_id,),
   230→        )
   231→        dependents = [dict(row) for row in cursor.fetchall()]
   232→
   233→        # Get recent events
   234→        events = self.db.get_events(issue_id=issue_id, limit=10)
   235→
   236→        # Parse tags from JSON
   237→        issue_dict = dict(issue)
   238→        self._parse_tags(issue_dict)
   239→
   240→        return {
   241→            **issue_dict,
   242→            "dependencies": dependencies,
   243→            "dependents": dependents,
   244→            "recent_events": events,
   245→        }

   247→    @cli_command(formatter=_fmt_message)
   248→    def update(
   249→        self,
   250→        issue_id: str,
   251→        title: Optional[str] = None,
   252→        description: Optional[str] = None,
   253→        priority: Optional[int] = None,
   254→        status: Optional[str] = None,
   255→        model: Optional[str] = None,
   256→        tags: Optional[str] = None,
   257→    ):
   258→        """Update an issue."""
   259→        self._require_issue(issue_id)
   260→
   261→        tag_list = [t.strip() for t in tags.split(",")] if tags is not None else None
   262→
   263→        updates = []
   264→        params = []
   265→
   266→        if title is not None:
   267→            updates.append("title = ?")
   268→            params.append(title)
   269→        if description is not None:
   270→            updates.append("description = ?")
   271→            params.append(description)
   272→        if priority is not None:
   273→            updates.append("priority = ?")
   274→            params.append(priority)
   275→        if status is not None:
   276→            updates.append("status = ?")
   277→            params.append(status)
   278→            # When re-opening an issue, clear the assignee so it
   279→            # re-enters the ready queue and can be claimed by a new agent.
   280→            if status == "open":
   281→                updates.append("assignee = NULL")
   282→        if model is not None:
   283→            updates.append("model = ?")
   284→            params.append(model)
   285→        if tag_list is not None:
   286→            validated_tags = validate_tags(tag_list)
   287→            updates.append("tags = ?")
   288→            params.append(json.dumps(validated_tags))
   289→
   290→        if updates:
   291→            query = f"UPDATE issues SET {', '.join(updates)}, updated_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ?"
   292→            params.append(issue_id)
   293→            self.db.conn.execute(query, params)
   294→            self.db.conn.commit()
   295→
   296→            self.db.log_event(
   297→                issue_id,
   298→                None,
   299→                "updated",
   300→                {
   301→                    "fields": [
   302→                        k
   303→                        for k, v in [
   304→                            ("title", title),
   305→                            ("description", description),
   306→                            ("priority", priority),
   307→                            ("status", status),
   308→                            ("model", model),
   309→                        ]
   310→                        if v is not None
   311→                    ]
   312→                },
   313→            )
   314→
   315→        return {"issue_id": issue_id, "message": f"Updated issue {issue_id}"}

   317→    @cli_command(formatter=_fmt_message)
   318→    def cancel(self, issue_id: str, reason: str = ""):
   319→        """Cancel an issue."""
   320→        self._require_issue(issue_id)
   321→
   322→        self.db.update_issue_status(issue_id, "canceled")
   323→        self.db.log_event(issue_id, None, "canceled", {"reason": reason})
   324→
   325→        return {
   326→            "issue_id": issue_id,
   327→            "status": "canceled",
   328→            "reason": reason,
   329→            "message": f"Canceled issue {issue_id}",
   330→        }
   331→
   332→    @cli_command(formatter=_fmt_message)
   333→    def finalize(self, issue_id: str, resolution: str = ""):
   334→        """Finalize/close an issue."""
   335→        self._require_issue(issue_id)
   336→
   337→        self.db.update_issue_status(issue_id, "finalized")
   338→        # If this issue was sitting in the merge queue (manual review mode),
   339→        # mark those entries complete so they don't get processed later.
   340→        self.db.conn.execute(
   341→            """
   342→            UPDATE merge_queue
   343→            SET status = 'merged', completed_at = datetime('now')
   344→            WHERE issue_id = ? AND status IN ('queued', 'running')
   345→            """,
   346→            (issue_id,),
   347→        )
   348→        self.db.conn.commit()
   349→        self.db.log_event(issue_id, None, "finalized", {"resolution": resolution})
   350→
   351→        return {
   352→            "issue_id": issue_id,
   353→            "status": "finalized",
   354→            "resolution": resolution,
   355→            "message": f"Finalized issue {issue_id}",
   356→        }

   358→    @cli_command(formatter=_fmt_review)
   359→    def review(self, issue_id: str | None = None, limit: int = 20):
   360→        """List done issues that are pending finalization with review hints.
   361→
   362→        If issue_id is given, show review info for that specific issue regardless of status.
   363→        """
   364→        if issue_id:
   365→            cursor = self.db.conn.execute(

1→"""Issues mixin: create, query, claim, and transition issues."""
     2→
     3→import json
     4→import logging
     5→
     6→from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional
     7→
     8→from ..utils import generate_id
     9→from .core import validate_tags
    10→
    11→logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
    12→
    13→
    14→class IssuesMixin:
    15→    def try_transition_issue_status(
    16→        self,
    17→        issue_id: str,
    18→        *,
    19→        from_status: str,
    20→        to_status: str,
    21→        expected_assignee: Optional[str] = None,
    22→    ) -> bool:
    23→        """CAS-style issue status transition.
    24→
    25→        For transitions to 'open', clears assignee (INV-2).
    26→
    27→        Returns:
    28→            True if updated, False otherwise.
    29→        """
    30→        with self.transaction() as conn:
    31→            cursor = conn.execute(
    32→                """
    33→                UPDATE issues
    34→                SET status = ?,
    35→                    assignee = CASE WHEN ? = 'open' THEN NULL ELSE assignee END,
    36→                    updated_at = datetime('now'),
    37→                    closed_at = CASE WHEN ? IN ('done', 'finalized', 'canceled', 'escalated')
    38→                                     THEN datetime('now')
    39→                                     ELSE closed_at END
    40→                WHERE id = ?
    41→                  AND status = ?
    42→                  AND (? IS NULL OR assignee = ?)
    43→                """,
    44→                (
    45→                    to_status,
    46→                    to_status,
    47→                    to_status,
    48→                    issue_id,
    49→                    from_status,
    50→                    expected_assignee,
    51→                    expected_assignee,
    52→                ),
    53→            )
    54→            if cursor.rowcount != 1:
    55→                return False
    56→
    57→            # commit=False so the status transition + audit event commit atomically via
    58→            # the surrounding `transaction()` context manager.
    59→            self.log_event(
    60→                issue_id,
    61→                None,
    62→                f"status_{to_status}",
    63→                {"status": to_status, "from": from_status, "to": to_status},
    64→                commit=False,
    65→            )
    66→            return True
    67→
    68→    def create_issue(
    69→        self,
    70→        title: str,
    71→        description: str = "",
    72→        priority: int = 2,
    73→        issue_type: str = "task",
    74→        project: str = "",
    75→        parent_id: Optional[str] = None,
    76→        model: Optional[str] = None,
    77→        tags: Optional[list[str]] = None,
    78→        metadata: Optional[Dict[str, Any]] = None,
    79→        depends_on: Optional[list[str]] = None,
    80→    ) -> str:
    81→        """
    82→        Create a new issue.
    83→
    84→        Dependencies are wired in the same transaction as the INSERT so
    85→        the issue is never visible to get_ready_queue without its deps.
    86→
    87→        Args:
    88→            title: Issue title
    89→            description: Issue description
    90→            priority: Priority (0=critical, 4=low)
    91→            issue_type: Type (task, bug, feature, step, epic)
    92→            project: Project/repo name
    93→            parent_id: Parent issue ID (for epics)
    94→            model: Model to use for this issue (overrides global WORKER_MODEL)
    95→            tags: List of tags for the issue (validated against ALLOWED_TAGS)
    96→            metadata: Additional metadata dict
    97→            depends_on: List of issue IDs this issue depends on (blocks type)
    98→
    99→        Returns:
   100→            Generated issue ID
   101→        """
   102→        issue_id = generate_id("w")
   103→        metadata_json = json.dumps(metadata) if metadata else None
   104→
   105→        # Validate and serialize tags
   106→        tags_json = None
   107→        if tags:
   108→            validated_tags = validate_tags(tags)
   109→            tags_json = json.dumps(validated_tags)
   110→
   111→        with self.transaction() as conn:
   112→            conn.execute(
   113→                """
   114→                INSERT INTO issues (id, title, description, priority, type, project, parent_id, model, tags, metadata)
   115→                VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
   116→                """,
   117→                (
   118→                    issue_id,
   119→                    title,
   120→                    description,
   121→                    priority,
   122→                    issue_type,
   123→                    project,
   124→                    parent_id,
   125→                    model,
   126→                    tags_json,
   127→                    metadata_json,
   128→                ),
   129→            )
   130→
   131→            # Wire deps in the same transaction — the issue is never visible
   132→            # to get_ready_queue without its blocking dependencies.
   133→            if depends_on:
   134→                for dep_id in depends_on:
   135→                    conn.execute(
   136→                        "INSERT OR IGNORE INTO dependencies (issue_id, depends_on, type) VALUES (?, ?, 'blocks')",
   137→                        (issue_id, dep_id),
   138→                    )
   139→
   140→            self.log_event(issue_id, None, "created", {"title": title}, commit=False)
   141→
   142→        return issue_id
   143→
   144→    def get_ready_queue(self, project: Optional[str] = None, limit: Optional[int] = None) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
   145→        """
   146→        Query for ready work items.
   147→
   148→        Returns issues that are:
   149→        - status = 'open'
   150→        - assignee IS NULL
   151→        - All blocking dependencies are resolved (done/finalized/canceled)
   152→
   153→        Args:
   154→            project: Filter by project (optional)
   155→            limit: Maximum number of items to return
   156→
   157→        Returns:
   158→            List of issue dicts, ordered by priority then creation time
   159→        """
   160→        query = """
   161→            SELECT i.*
   162→            FROM issues i
   163→            WHERE i.status = 'open'
   164→              AND i.assignee IS NULL
   165→              AND NOT EXISTS (
   166→                SELECT 1 FROM dependencies d
   167→                JOIN issues blocker ON d.depends_on = blocker.id
   168→                WHERE d.issue_id = i.id
   169→                  AND d.type = 'blocks'
   170→                  AND blocker.status NOT IN ('done', 'finalized', 'canceled')
   171→              )
   172→        """
   173→
   174→        params = []
   175→        if project is not None:
   176→            query += " AND i.project = ?"
   177→            params.append(project)
   178→
   179→        query += " ORDER BY i.priority ASC, i.created_at ASC"
   180→
   181→        if limit:
   182→            query += f" LIMIT {limit}"
   183→
   184→        cursor = self.conn.execute(query, params)
   185→        return [dict(row) for row in cursor.fetchall()]
   186→
   187→    def claim_issue(self, issue_id: str, agent_id: str) -> bool:
   188→        """
   189→        Atomically claim an issue using CAS (Compare-And-Set).
   190→
   191→        Only succeeds if the issue is unclaimed AND all blocking dependencies
   192→        are resolved. This prevents a race where the orchestrator grabs an issue
   193→        from the ready queue before its dependencies have been wired.
   194→
   195→        Args:
   196→            issue_id: ID of issue to claim
   197→            agent_id: ID of agent claiming the issue
   198→
   199→        Returns:
   200→            True if claim successful, False if already claimed or blocked
   201→        """
   202→        with self.transaction() as conn:
   203→            cursor = conn.execute(
   204→                """
   205→                UPDATE issues
   206→                SET assignee = ?,
   207→                    status = 'in_progress',
   208→                    updated_at = datetime('now')
   209→                WHERE id = ?
   210→                  AND assignee IS NULL
   211→                  AND NOT EXISTS (
   212→                    SELECT 1 FROM dependencies d
   213→                    JOIN issues blocker ON d.depends_on = blocker.id
   214→                    WHERE d.issue_id = ?
   215→                      AND d.type = 'blocks'
   216→                      AND blocker.status NOT IN ('done', 'finalized', 'canceled')
   217→                  )
   218→                """,
   219→                (agent_id, issue_id, issue_id),
   220→            )
   221→
   222→            success = cursor.rowcount == 1
   223→
   224→            if success:
   225→                # Update agent's current issue
   226→                conn.execute(
   227→                    """
   228→                    UPDATE agents
   229→                    SET current_issue = ?,
   230→                        status = 'working',
   231→                        updated_at = datetime('now')
   232→                    WHERE id = ?
   233→                    """,
   234→                    (issue_id, agent_id),
   235→                )
   236→                self.log_event(issue_id, agent_id, "claimed", {}, commit=False)
   237→
   238→            return success
   239→
   240→    def get_issue(self, issue_id: str) -> Optional[Dict[str, Any]]:
   241→        """Get issue by ID."""
   242→        cursor = self.conn.execute("SELECT * FROM issues WHERE id = ?", (issue_id,))
   243→        row = cursor.fetchone()
   244→        return dict(row) if row else None
   245→
   246→    def update_issue_status(self, issue_id: str, status: str):
   247→        """Update issue status. Clears assignee when setting to 'open' (INV-2)."""
   248→        with self.transaction() as conn:
   249→            conn.execute(
   250→                """
   251→                UPDATE issues
   252→                SET status = ?,
   253→                    assignee = CASE WHEN ? = 'open' THEN NULL ELSE assignee END,
   254→                    updated_at = datetime('now'),
   255→                    closed_at = CASE WHEN ? IN ('done', 'finalized', 'canceled', 'escalated')
   256→                                     THEN datetime('now')
   257→                                     ELSE closed_at END
   258→                WHERE id = ?
   259→                """,
   260→                (status, status, status, issue_id),
   261→            )
   262→            self.log_event(issue_id, None, f"status_{status}", {"status": status}, commit=False)
   263→
   264→    def add_dependency(self, issue_id: str, depends_on: str, dep_type: str = "blocks"):
   265→        """Add a dependency between issues."""
   266→        with self.transaction() as conn:
   267→            conn.execute(
   268→                """
   269→                INSERT OR IGNORE INTO dependencies (issue_id, depends_on, type)
   270→                VALUES (?, ?, ?)
   271→                """,
   272→                (issue_id, depends_on, dep_type),
   273→            )
   274→

1→"""Notes mixin: note CRUD and delivery tracking."""
     2→
     3→import logging
     4→
     5→from typing import Any, Dict, List, Optional, Tuple
     6→
     7→logger = logging.getLogger(__name__)
     8→
     9→
    10→class NotesMixin:
    11→    def add_note(
    12→        self,
    13→        issue_id: Optional[str] = None,
    14→        agent_id: Optional[str] = None,
    15→        content: str = "",
    16→        category: str = "discovery",
    17→        project: Optional[str] = None,
    18→        must_read: bool = False,
    19→    ) -> int:
    20→        """
    21→        Insert a note and return its ID.
    22→
    23→        Args:
    24→            issue_id: Which issue the note was discovered during. None = project-wide note.
    25→            agent_id: Which agent wrote it. None = Queen-authored or system note.
    26→            content: The note text. Short — typically 1-3 sentences.
    27→            category: One of 'discovery', 'gotcha', 'dependency', 'pattern', 'context'.
    28→            project: Project identifier. If None and issue_id provided, backfilled via migration.
    29→            must_read: If True, recipients must acknowledge this note before proceeding.
    30→
    31→        Returns:
    32→            The ID of the inserted note
    33→        """
    34→        if not self.conn:
    35→            raise RuntimeError("Database not connected")
    36→
    37→        cursor = self.conn.execute(
    38→            "INSERT INTO notes (issue_id, agent_id, category, content, project, must_read) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)",
    39→            (issue_id, agent_id, category, content, project, 1 if must_read else 0),
    40→        )
    41→        self.conn.commit()
    42→        return cursor.lastrowid
    43→
    44→    def get_notes(
    45→        self, issue_id: Optional[str] = None, category: Optional[str] = None, project: Optional[str] = None, limit: int = 20
    46→    ) -> List[Dict]:
    47→        """
    48→        Retrieve notes with optional filtering. Returns newest first.
    49→
    50→        Args:
    51→            issue_id: Filter by specific issue ID (optional)
    52→            category: Filter by specific category (optional)
    53→            project: Filter by project (optional). NULL-project notes match any query for backward compat.
    54→            limit: Maximum number of notes to return
    55→
    56→        Returns:
    57→            List of note dicts, ordered by newest first
    58→        """
    59→        if not self.conn:
    60→            raise RuntimeError("Database not connected")
    61→
    62→        query = "SELECT * FROM notes WHERE 1=1"
    63→        params = []
    64→        if issue_id is not None:
    65→            query += " AND issue_id = ?"
    66→            params.append(issue_id)
    67→        if category is not None:
    68→            query += " AND category = ?"
    69→            params.append(category)
    70→        if project is not None:
    71→            query += " AND (project = ? OR project IS NULL)"
    72→            params.append(project)
    73→        query += " ORDER BY created_at DESC LIMIT ?"
    74→        params.append(limit)
    75→        rows = self.conn.execute(query, params).fetchall()
    76→        return [dict(row) for row in rows]
    77→
    78→    def create_note_deliveries(
    79→        self,
    80→        note_id: int,
    81→        to_agents: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    82→        to_issues: Optional[List[str]] = None,
    83→    ) -> int:
    84→        """
    85→        Create delivery rows for a note.
    86→
    87→        For each agent in to_agents: inserts an agent-global delivery (recipient_issue_id=NULL).
    88→        For each issue in to_issues: inserts an issue-following target row (recipient_agent_id=NULL).
    89→        Uses INSERT OR IGNORE to deduplicate based on unique indexes.
    90→
    91→        Returns:
    92→            Count of rows actually inserted.
    93→        """
    94→        if not self.conn:
    95→            raise RuntimeError("Database not connected")
    96→
    97→        inserted = 0
    98→        with self.transaction() as conn:
    99→            for agent_id in to_agents or []:
   100→                cursor = conn.execute(
   101→                    "INSERT OR IGNORE INTO note_deliveries (note_id, recipient_agent_id, recipient_issue_id) VALUES (?, ?, NULL)",
   102→                    (note_id, agent_id),
   103→                )
   104→                inserted += cursor.rowcount
   105→            for issue_id in to_issues or []:
   106→                cursor = conn.execute(
   107→                    "INSERT OR IGNORE INTO note_deliveries (note_id, recipient_agent_id, recipient_issue_id) VALUES (?, NULL, ?)",
   108→                    (note_id, issue_id),
   109→                )
   110→                inserted += cursor.rowcount
   111→        return inserted
   112→
   113→    def materialize_issue_deliveries(self, issue_id: str, agent_id: str, project: str) -> int:
   114→        """
   115→        Materialize issue-following target rows into concrete agent deliveries.
   116→
   117→        Finds all note_deliveries rows with recipient_issue_id=issue_id and
   118→        recipient_agent_id IS NULL (issue-following targets), then inserts a
   119→        concrete delivery row for the given agent+issue pair for each.
   120→
   121→        Returns:
   122→            Count of rows actually inserted.
   123→        """
   124→        if not self.conn:
   125→            raise RuntimeError("Database not connected")
   126→
   127→        rows = self.conn.execute(
   128→            "SELECT note_id FROM note_deliveries WHERE recipient_issue_id = ? AND recipient_agent_id IS NULL",
   129→            (issue_id,),
   130→        ).fetchall()
   131→
   132→        inserted = 0
   133→        with self.transaction() as conn:
   134→            for row in rows:
   135→                cursor = conn.execute(
   136→                    "INSERT OR IGNORE INTO note_deliveries (note_id, recipient_agent_id, recipient_issue_id) VALUES (?, ?, ?)",
   137→                    (row["note_id"], agent_id, issue_id),
   138→                )
   139→                inserted += cursor.rowcount
   140→        return inserted
   141→
   142→    def get_injectable_deliveries(
   143→        self,
   144→        agent_id: str,
   145→        issue_id: str,
   146→        project: str,
   147→        max_normal: int = 5,
   148→    ) -> Tuple[List[Dict[str, Any]], bool]:
   149→        """
   150→        Query deliveries eligible for worker turn injection.
   151→
   152→        Includes:
   153→        - must_read notes: while status != 'acked'
   154→        - normal notes: while status IN ('queued', 'delivered')
   155→
   156→        Ordering: must_read DESC, queued before delivered, then created_at ASC.
   157→        Caps normal deliveries at max_normal; always includes all must_read.
   158→
   159→        Returns:
   160→            (deliveries_list, has_more_bool)
   161→        """
   162→        if not self.conn:
   163→            raise RuntimeError("Database not connected")
   164→
   165→        rows = self.conn.execute(
   166→            """
   167→            SELECT
   168→                nd.id AS delivery_id,
   169→                nd.note_id,
   170→                n.content,
   171→                n.must_read,
   172→                nd.status,
   173→                n.agent_id AS from_agent_id,
   174→                CASE WHEN nd.recipient_issue_id IS NULL THEN 'agent' ELSE 'issue' END AS scope,
   175→                nd.recipient_issue_id
   176→            FROM note_deliveries nd
   177→            JOIN notes n ON nd.note_id = n.id
   178→            WHERE nd.recipient_agent_id = ?
   179→              AND (nd.recipient_issue_id IS NULL OR nd.recipient_issue_id = ?)
   180→              AND (
   181→                (n.must_read = 1 AND nd.status != 'acked')
   182→                OR
   183→                (n.must_read = 0 AND nd.status IN ('queued', 'delivered'))
   184→              )
   185→            ORDER BY
   186→                n.must_read DESC,
   187→                CASE nd.status WHEN 'queued' THEN 0 WHEN 'delivered' THEN 1 ELSE 2 END ASC,
   188→                nd.created_at ASC
   189→            """,
   190→            (agent_id, issue_id),
   191→        ).fetchall()
   192→
   193→        must_read_rows = [dict(r) for r in rows if r["must_read"]]
   194→        normal_rows = [dict(r) for r in rows if not r["must_read"]]
   195→
   196→        has_more = len(normal_rows) > max_normal
   197→        selected = must_read_rows + normal_rows[:max_normal]
   198→        return selected, has_more
   199→
   200→    def mark_delivery_delivered(self, delivery_id: int) -> bool:
   201→        """
   202→        Transition a delivery from 'queued' to 'delivered'.
   203→
   204→        Returns True if the row was updated (i.e., it was in 'queued' state).
   205→        """
   206→        if not self.conn:
   207→            raise RuntimeError("Database not connected")
   208→
   209→        with self.transaction() as conn:
   210→            cursor = conn.execute(
   211→                "UPDATE note_deliveries SET status = 'delivered', delivered_at = datetime('now') WHERE id = ? AND status = 'queued'",
   212→                (delivery_id,),
   213→            )
   214→        return cursor.rowcount == 1
   215→
   216→    def mark_delivery_read(self, delivery_id: int, agent_id: str) -> bool:
   217→        """
   218→        Transition a delivery to 'read'. Only valid if status is 'queued' or 'delivered'.
   219→
   220→        Returns True if the row was updated.
   221→        """
   222→        if not self.conn:
   223→            raise RuntimeError("Database not connected")
   224→
   225→        with self.transaction() as conn:
   226→            cursor = conn.execute(
   227→                """
   228→                UPDATE note_deliveries
   229→                SET status = 'read', read_at = datetime('now')
   230→                WHERE id = ? AND recipient_agent_id = ? AND status IN ('queued', 'delivered')
   231→                """,
   232→                (delivery_id, agent_id),
   233→            )
   234→        return cursor.rowcount == 1
   235→
   236→    def mark_delivery_acked(self, delivery_id: int, agent_id: str) -> bool:
   237→        """
   238→        Acknowledge a must_read delivery. Only valid for notes with must_read=1.
   239→
   240→        Returns True if the row was updated. Returns False if the note is not must_read.
   241→        """
   242→        if not self.conn:
   243→            raise RuntimeError("Database not connected")
   244→
   245→        with self.transaction() as conn:
   246→            cursor = conn.execute(
   247→                """
   248→                UPDATE note_deliveries
   249→                SET status = 'acked', acked_at = datetime('now')
   250→                WHERE id = ?
   251→                  AND recipient_agent_id = ?
   252→                  AND note_id IN (SELECT id FROM notes WHERE must_read = 1)
   253→                """,
   254→                (delivery_id, agent_id),
   255→            )
   256→        return cursor.rowcount == 1
   257→
   258→    def get_required_unacked_deliveries(self, agent_id: str, issue_id: str) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
   259→        """
   260→        Return all must_read deliveries that have not been acked for the given agent/issue.
   261→
   262→        Covers both agent-global (recipient_issue_id IS NULL) and issue-scoped deliveries.
   263→
   264→        Returns:
   265→            List of dicts with delivery_id, note_id, content, status.
   266→        """
   267→        if not self.conn:
   268→            raise RuntimeError("Database not connected")
   269→
   270→        rows = self.conn.execute(
   271→            """
   272→            SELECT
   273→                nd.id AS delivery_id,
   274→                nd.note_id,
   275→                n.content,
   276→                nd.status
   277→            FROM note_deliveries nd
   278→            JOIN notes n ON nd.note_id = n.id
   279→            WHERE n.must_read = 1
   280→              AND nd.status != 'acked'
   281→              AND nd.recipient_agent_id = ?
   282→              AND (nd.recipient_issue_id IS NULL OR nd.recipient_issue_id = ?)
   283→            ORDER BY nd.created_at ASC
   284→            """,
   285→            (agent_id, issue_id),
   286→        ).fetchall()
   287→        return [dict(r) for r in rows]
   288→
   289→    def get_inbox_deliveries(
   290→        self,
   291→        agent_id: str,
   292→        issue_id: Optional[str] = None,
   293→        unread_only: bool = False,
   294→    ) -> List[Dict[str, Any]]:
   295→        """
   296→        Query deliveries for CLI hive mail inbox.
   297→
   298→        Args:
   299→            agent_id: Filter deliveries for this agent.
   300→            issue_id: If provided, further filter by issue scope.
   301→            unread_only: If True, exclude status='read' and status='acked'.
   302→
   303→        Returns:
   304→            List of delivery dicts ordered by created_at DESC.
   305→        """
   306→        if not self.conn:
   307→            raise RuntimeError("Database not connected")
   308→
   309→        query = """
   310→            SELECT
   311→                nd.*,
   312→                n.content,
   313→                n.must_read,
   314→                n.agent_id AS from_agent_id
   315→            FROM note_deliveries nd
   316→            JOIN notes n ON nd.note_id = n.id
   317→            WHERE nd.recipient_agent_id = ?
   318→        """
   319→        params: List[Any] = [agent_id]
   320→
   321→        if issue_id is not None:
   322→            query += " AND nd.recipient_issue_id = ?"
   323→            params.append(issue_id)
   324→
   325→        if unread_only:
   326→            query += " AND nd.status NOT IN ('read', 'acked')"
   327→
   328→        query += " ORDER BY nd.created_at DESC"
   329→
   330→        rows = self.conn.execute(query, params).fetchall()
   331→        return [dict(r) for r in rows]
   332→

   814→    @staticmethod
   815→    def _event_to_json(event: dict) -> dict:
   816→        """Prepare an event dict for JSON serialisation.
   817→
   818→        Parses the ``detail`` field from a JSON string into a real object
   819→        so the output is a proper nested structure rather than an escaped
   820→        string.
   821→        """
   822→        out = dict(event)
   823→        if out.get("detail"):
   824→            try:
   825→                out["detail"] = json.loads(out["detail"]) if isinstance(out["detail"], str) else out["detail"]
   826→            except (json.JSONDecodeError, TypeError):
   827→                pass  # keep as-is
   828→        return out

   830→    @cli_command(formatter=_fmt_logs)
   831→    def logs(
   832→        self,
   833→        follow: bool = False,
   834→        n: int = 20,
   835→        issue_id: Optional[str] = None,
   836→        agent_id: Optional[str] = None,
   837→        event_type: Optional[str] = None,
   838→        daemon: bool = False,
   839→    ):
   840→        """Show event log, optionally tailing for new events."""
   841→        # --daemon flag: delegate to daemon log handler (handles output itself)
   842→        if daemon:
   843→            daemon_obj = self._make_daemon()
   844→            daemon_obj.logs(lines=n, follow=follow)
   845→            return None
   846→
   847→        recent = self.db.get_recent_events(n=n, issue_id=issue_id, agent_id=agent_id, event_type=event_type)
   848→
   849→        # Follow/streaming mode: handle output inline, return None to skip decorator output.
   850→        # Uses self._json_mode (set by @cli_command decorator) to choose text vs JSONL.
   851→        if follow:
   852→            json_mode = self._json_mode
   853→            for event in recent:
   854→                if json_mode:
   855→                    print(json.dumps(self._event_to_json(event), default=str))
   856→                else:
   857→                    print(self._format_event(event))
   858→            cursor = recent[-1]["id"] if recent else self.db.get_max_event_id()
   859→            try:
   860→                while True:
   861→                    time.sleep(0.5)
   862→                    new_events = self.db.get_events_since(after_id=cursor, issue_id=issue_id, agent_id=agent_id, event_type=event_type)
   863→                    for event in new_events:
   864→                        if json_mode:
   865→                            print(json.dumps(self._event_to_json(event), default=str))
   866→                        else:
   867→                            print(self._format_event(event))
   868→                        cursor = event["id"]
   869→            except KeyboardInterrupt:
   870→                pass
   871→            return None
   872→
   873→        # Non-follow: return events for decorator to format/serialize
   874→        return {"events": [dict(e) for e in recent]}
   875→

   883→    @cli_command(formatter=_fmt_metrics)
   884→    def metrics(self, model=None, tag=None, issue_type=None, group_by=None, show_costs=False, issue_id=None, agent_id=None):
   885→        """Show aggregated agent run metrics."""
   886→        # If --group-by is specified, use the stats-style output
   887→        if group_by:
   888→            results = self.db.get_model_performance(model=model, tag=tag, group_by=group_by)
   889→            group_label = "Tag" if group_by == "tag" else "Type"
   890→            group_key = "tag" if group_by == "tag" else "type"
   891→            return {"view": "group_by", "results": results, "group_label": group_label, "group_key": group_key}
   892→
   893→        # If --costs is specified, show token usage data
   894→        if show_costs:
   895→            usage = self.db.get_token_usage(issue_id=issue_id, agent_id=agent_id, project=self.project_name)
   896→            return {"view": "costs", "issue_id": issue_id, "agent_id": agent_id, **usage}
   897→
   898→        # Default metrics output
   899→        results = self.db.get_metrics(model=model, tag=tag, issue_type=issue_type, project=self.project_name)
   900→
   901→        total_runs = sum(r["runs"] for r in results)
   902→        total_escalations = sum(r["escalated_count"] for r in results)
   903→        escalation_rate = round(100.0 * total_escalations / total_runs, 1) if total_runs > 0 else 0
   904→        total_duration_weighted = sum(r["avg_duration_s"] * r["runs"] for r in results if r["avg_duration_s"])
   905→        mean_duration_s = total_duration_weighted / total_runs if total_runs > 0 else 0
   906→        mean_duration_m = round(mean_duration_s / 60, 1)
   907→
   908→        return {
   909→            "view": "default",
   910→            "metrics": results,
   911→            "summary": {
   912→                "escalation_rate": escalation_rate,
   913→                "mean_time_to_resolution_minutes": mean_duration_m,
   914→                "total_runs": total_runs,
   915→            },
   916→        }

   920→    def _make_daemon(self) -> HiveDaemon:
   921→        return HiveDaemon(db_path=self.db.db_path)

   923→    @cli_command(formatter=_fmt_start)
   924→    def start(self, foreground: bool = False):
   925→        """Start the hive daemon."""
   926→        if foreground:
   927→            from ..daemon import run_daemon_foreground
   928→
   929→            run_daemon_foreground(self.db)
   930→            return None
   931→
   932→        daemon = self._make_daemon()
   933→        status = daemon.status()
   934→        if status["running"]:
   935→            return {"status": "already_running", "pid": status["pid"]}
   936→
   937→        started = daemon.start()
   938→        if started:
   939→            ds = daemon.status()
   940→            return {"status": "started", "pid": ds["pid"], "log_file": ds.get("log_file")}
   941→
   942→        log_tail = ""
   943→        try:
   944→            if daemon.log_file.exists():
   945→                lines = daemon.log_file.read_text().strip().splitlines()
   946→                log_tail = "\n".join(lines[-10:])
   947→        except OSError:
   948→            pass
   949→        raise RuntimeError(f"Failed to start daemon. Log: {daemon.log_file}\n{log_tail}".rstrip())
   950→
   951→    @cli_command(formatter=_fmt_stop)
   952→    def stop(self):
   953→        """Stop the hive daemon."""
   954→        daemon = self._make_daemon()
   955→        status = daemon.status()
   956→        if not status["running"]:
   957→            return {"status": "not_running"}
   958→        pid = status["pid"]
   959→        stopped = daemon.stop()
   960→        if stopped:
   961→            return {"status": "stopped", "pid": pid}
   962→        raise RuntimeError(f"Failed to stop daemon (PID {pid})")
